1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## Search Protocol
73	
74	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
75	
76	1. `lean_local_search` first
77	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
78	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
79	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
80	
81	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
82	
83	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
84	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
85	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
86	
87	## Tooling Traps & Verification
88	
89	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
90	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
91	
92	## Logging
93	
94	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
95	
96	**Format:**
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
100	
101	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
102	### Attempt 1
103	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
104	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
105	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
106	
107	### Attempt 2
108	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
109	- **Result:** RESOLVED
110	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
111	
112	## helper_bijective (line 78)
113	### Attempt 1
114	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
115	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
116	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
117	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
118	```
119	
120	**Rules:**
121	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
122	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
123	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
124	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
125	
126	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
127	
128	## Summary Pipeline
129	
130	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
131	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
132	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
133	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
134	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
135	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
136	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
137	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
138	
139	## End-of-session handoff
140	
141	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
142	
143	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
144	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
145	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
146	   - Concrete next step for the next session
147	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
148	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
149

319	## Forbidden shortcuts (documented once)
320	
321	Standing rules:
322	- No new `axiom` declarations.
323	- No constant/vacuous shortcuts for protected definitions.
324	- No `≃` / `≃+` where `≃ₗ[k]` is required.
325	- Use `theorem` (not `instance`) for consumers taking explicit non-synthesisable arguments.
326	- Avoid `Scheme.` short-name prefix inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`.
327	- Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ _` *unconditionally* — forbidden.
328	- Plan-agent must not pre-verify candidate proof bodies via `lean_run_code` / `lean_multi_attempt`; no chains of thin helper extraction across iterations.
329	- Future code opening `MonObj` must avoid `ι` as a binder name (scoped Mathlib notation for `GrpObj.inv`).
330	- **iter-086**: No helper with a universally-false signature may be introduced even with a `sorry` body. Example violation (iter-085): `lemma foo (S : ShortComplex ...) : S.Exact := sorry` claims every short complex is exact. When the genuine `iff`-form is not closeable, leave the inline `sorry` rather than displacing it via a `sorry`-bodied helper.
331	- **iter-086**: No 80+-line in-code comment blocks documenting dead-end tactics; if the lane stalls, write a SHORT task report and escalate to refactor rather than expanding the inline scaffolding.
332	- **iter-087**: When extracting an inline `have` to a top-level decl via refactor, the extracted signature MUST be concrete to the call site's specialized context. iter-087 first-pass `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` had a universally-false signature; specialize pass caught it.
333	- **iter-089**: When committing a multi-step tactic chain (here S6 = a,b,c,…h) without LSP feedback, do NOT wrap the whole chain in `first | <chain> | sorry` — that loses the "deepest committed step" signal needed for next-iter re-scoping. Commit each step linearly and put the trailing `sorry` after the deepest committed step.
334	- **iter-090**: For multi-step tactic chains under LSP-unavailable conditions, prefer universal `simp only [map_sum]` over direct `rw [foo.map_sum, …]` for AddMonoidHomClass distribution — `simp only` tolerates HOU pattern variants and iterates through nested wrappers (`eqToHom`, `Function.comp`, etc.). Also: when both sides of an equation are sums but the RHS has `r • ∑` shape, prefer forward-direction `simp only [Finset.smul_sum]` BEFORE `Finset.sum_congr` over the original end-of-chain `rw [← Finset.smul_sum]` — mathematically equivalent, structurally cleaner. The per-step fallback ladder is now refined to per-step granularity (each step in the chain is its own commit point).
335	- **iter-092**: `sorry_analyzer.py` counts syntactic `sorry` tokens but does NOT verify compilation. When mathlib was missing in the sandbox (iter-086 → iter-091), only `sorry_analyzer.py` ran for verification, and false positives accumulated. **Plan-agent verification step: ALWAYS run `lake build <target>` or `lean_diagnostic_messages` as part of the verification pass; do NOT trust `sorry_analyzer.py` alone for compilation status.** When the sandbox is missing mathlib, `lake exe cache get` from project root + `$HOME/.elan/bin` on PATH fetches it; this works in iter-092+. The prover under LSP-unavailable conditions should NOT commit tactic chains beyond a single step — the iter-091 bare-commit-without-LSP pattern stacks unverified work and produces false-positive sorry counts.
336	- **iter-092**: `letI x : T := v` in CONCLUSION position of a `theorem ... :  ... := by ...` is silently zeta-reduced AWAY during elaboration if `x` doesn't appear explicitly in the rest of the conclusion. The instance synthesis hint provided by the letI is BAKED INTO downstream `•`/`*`/`⬝`-style instance uses, but the let-binder `x` does NOT survive. `intro` cannot name what isn't there. **Idiom for the pattern "introduce instances in the statement so the conclusion typechecks, then access them by name in the body":** move the `letI` block from conclusion-position to body-position (right after `intro` of the let-binders that the letI bodies depend on). Both forms compute the same baked-in instance; the body-position form keeps the name in scope for downstream tactics.
337	- **iter-093**: When `simp only [lem]` / `rw [lem]` fails with "made no progress" or "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" on a goal that visibly contains the LHS of `lem`, the cause is usually HOU pattern matching failure (the summand or closure body references the bound index in two or more positions). Standard escalation ladder: (1) try `simp_rw [lem]` first — Lean's `simp_rw` is more tolerant of HOU patterns than `simp only`; (2) if `simp_rw` also fails, build the equation manually via an explicit `AddMonoidHom` / `RingHom` / `LinearMap` and apply `map_sum` / `map_mul` / `map_smul` — these `map_*` lemmas operate on the structure homomorphism rather than via discrimination-tree HOU; (3) as a last resort, use `Finset.cons_induction` with per-step `Finset.sum_cons` + `map_add` (or the relevant additivity lemma) + IH.
338	- **iter-094**: `LinearMap.comp_apply` (`rfl`-lemma: `(f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x)`) does NOT fire if `g`'s codomain and `f`'s source are def-equal but NOT syntactically equal. Symptoms: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "no progress"; `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "pattern `(?f ∘ₗ ?g) ?x` not found"; `rw [show (f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x) from rfl]` reports same. Cause: the discrimination tree's unification fails to identify two def-equal-but-syntactically-distinct types (e.g. `∏ᶜ Z₂` vs `ModuleCat.of k (Pi Z₂)`). **Escalation ladder**: (1) try `LinearMap.ext` to lift the equality OUT of the `∘ₗ`-application — at the `intro x` binder, Lean elaborates the codomain explicitly, bypassing the discrimination tree; (2) eliminate the source of the mismatch (e.g., drop the simp lemma that introduced the def-equal unfolding from the simp set; replace with explicit `Eq.mpr` cast); (3) restructure to push the offending wrapping outside the sum at a higher level of abstraction (categorical, not LinearMap).
339	- **iter-095**: When iter-094's `LinearMap.comp_apply` failure is structural (def-equal-not-syntactic-equal codomain/source), the iter-094 escape via `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` (`(f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom` IN REVERSE) FUSES the `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` LinearMap composition into the categorical `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` — at the categorical level, `≫` accepts def-equal types where the LinearMap discrimination tree did not. **Pattern**: when a LinearMap `f ∘ₗ g` rewrite is HOU-blocked, try fusing into a categorical `g' ≫ f'` via `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` (or analogous `← Functor.map_comp`-style lemmas for other categories).
340	- **iter-095**: HOU pattern matching on a `Preadditive.sum_comp` / `key₂`-style lemma where the summand body has nested references to the outer summation index in non-Miller positions (e.g., `(-1)^↑i`, `SimplexCategory.δ i` applied multiple times) is fundamentally blocked. The lemma may be provable (HOU-friendly proof with free variables) but APPLYING it via `rw`/`simp` fails. **Escalation ladder**: (1) `set F : Fin (n+1) → (X ⟶ Y) := fun i => <summand body> with hF`; the `set` substitutes the body with `F i` in the goal, so subsequent `rw`s have no HOU. Type ascription is CRITICAL — for `(-1)^↑i • <body>`, use `(((-1 : ℤ))^(i : ℕ) : ℤ) • <body>` with explicit casts. May need a preceding `change`/`show` to fold let-bound symbols (e.g. `Z₁`) back from their unfolded form. (2) Push the offending wrapping outside via a second `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` to absorb outer `(Pi.π ...)`-style factors, then apply the distribution with an `i`-independent `?g`. (3) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` to peel summands manually — each cons-step has the fresh index as a HYPOTHESIS (not a binder), so HOU vanishes. **iter-095 EXTENSION** (iter-096 outcome): ALL THREE tactic-level routes proved structurally blocked in practice — (G) `set F` fails because `change`/`show`-fold of `Z₁` would need to match an eta-mismatched literal form; (H) `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` / `← CategoryTheory.comp_apply` fail on def-equal-not-syntactic Pi-product / unfolded carrier types; (I) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` fails typeclass synthesis (`Fintype ?m`). When all three fail, the route forward is to **extract an abstract structural lemma whose summand family is a single lemma-level binder `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)`**: applying that lemma uses first-order Miller unification (`?F := fun i ↦ <summand body>`) — fundamentally distinct from `rw [key₂]`'s discrimination-tree HOU, which fails on the same summand.
341	- **iter-096**: When a refactor agent tries to bake a complex `let φ_i := ...` expression into a lemma's SIGNATURE, and the body of the `let` contains type-level coercions like `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` that need to unify with `Fin n → ↥s₀` (the body's actual type post-`dif_pos hRel`), Lean's elaborator will deterministically `whnf` timeout (no tactic state to apply `dif_pos`). **Workaround pattern**: state the helper at the simpler index type and let the call site rewrite to bridge; OR keep the summand body abstract (as a lemma binder rather than a `let`-binding); OR inline the proof entirely instead of factoring through the helper. The iter-096 refactor confirmed: abstract structural lemmas (with the family `F` as a binder) elaborate cleanly; per-summand helpers with literal Čech bodies baked in their signatures do not.
342	- **iter-096**: When iter-095's tactic-level routes (G/H/I) ALL fail with structural reasons documented at the LSP level, the iter-096 escalation pattern is: extract an abstract structural lemma whose conclusion's UNIFICATION against the goal is Miller-friendly. The lemma's body is a separate proof obligation (sorry-filled by next-iter prover), and so is the per-summand discharge at the call site. This trades 1 syntactic sorry for 2 syntactic sorries — but EACH is HOU-free because the family is named, so the prover can fill both routinely. The iter-094 `key₂` body-local helper had the same intent but body-locally; promoting it to top-level moves the application's HOU into first-order Miller unification (the Lean kernel handles it).

777	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
778	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
779	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
780	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
781	schemes in our setting.
782	
783	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
784	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
785	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
786	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
787	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
788	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
789	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
790	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
791	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
792	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
793	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
794	
795	The structural skeleton is:
796	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
797	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
798	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
799	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.

400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology

412	/-- Iter-087 (refactor): per-summand R-linear restriction-collapse. For nested
413	opens `V ≤ W ≤ U` in a scheme `C.left`, the chain of presheaf restrictions
414	`Γ(U) → Γ(W) → Γ(V)` collapses to the direct restriction `Γ(U) → Γ(V)`.
415	
416	Used in the proof of R-linearity of the Čech differential
417	(`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`): each per-summand restriction chain
418	`R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(C.left, V_j)` collapses to the
419	direct map `R → Γ(C.left, V_j)`, matching the per-i R-action induced by the
420	restriction algebra structure on the cochain factor.
421	
422	Extracted iter-087 from the inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` (iter-086) inside
423	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. The body is the
424	2-tactic proof verified by the iter-086 prover. -/
425	lemma presheafMap_restrict_collapse
426	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
427	    {V W U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
428	    (h_VW : V ≤ W) (h_VU : V ≤ U) (h_WU : W ≤ U)
429	    (r : C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U)) :
430	    (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VW).op).hom
431	        ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_WU).op).hom r) =
432	      (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VU).op).hom r := by
433	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
434	  congr 1

437	/-- Iter-096 (refactor extraction): structural lemma — if each summand
438	`F i` is R-linear (with respect to the LinearEquivs `e₁`, `e₂`), then
439	so is `∑ i ∈ s, F i`. The summand family `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)` is named
440	abstractly, so applying this lemma to the Čech alternating sum
441	`∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` only requires Miller-pattern
442	unification `F := fun i ↦ <literal summand>` — no HOU on the
443	nested-i body.
444	
445	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-097 prover. Proof sketch:
446	induct on the finset `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Base case is
447	the empty sum = 0, which is trivially R-linear via the `ModuleCat`
448	zero morphism. Cons step: `F i + ∑ j ∈ s', F j` is R-linear because
449	R-linearity is preserved under addition (`ModuleCat.hom_add` plus
450	R-linearity of each summand).
451	
452	**Refactor note (iter-096):** The directive also requested a companion
453	`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand` that bakes the Čech summand literal
454	into its signature. That signature triggers a whnf timeout at the
455	`Pi.lift (fun x ↦ Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i)
456	.toOrderHom x))`-elaboration step, because Lean attempts to unify
457	`Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` with `Fin n → ↥s₀` through
458	the heavy `ComplexShape.prev` definition. We omit that helper in iter-096;
459	the abstract structural lemma below suffices to give the iter-097 prover a
460	HOU-friendly hook (instantiate `F := fun i ↦ <Čech summand>` and discharge
461	per-summand R-linearity inline). -/
462	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
463	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
464	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
465	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
466	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
467	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
468	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
469	    (F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
470	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
471	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
472	    (hF : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
473	      e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
474	        r • e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
475	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
476	      e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
477	        r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
478	  -- iter-097 Step 1: induct on `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Empty case
479	  -- collapses via `ModuleCat.hom_zero`; cons step distributes `Finset.sum_cons`
480	  -- + `ModuleCat.hom_add` + `LinearMap.add_apply` + `map_add` simultaneously
481	  -- on both sides via `simp only`, then applies `hF` for the head and `ih`
482	  -- for the tail, finishing with `smul_add`. HOU-free because `F` is a binder.
483	  revert hF
484	  induction s using Finset.cons_induction with
485	  | empty =>
486	    intro _hF r y
487	    simp [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply,
488	      map_zero, smul_zero]
489	  | cons i s' hi ih =>
490	    intro hF r y
491	    simp only [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]
492	    rw [hF i (Finset.mem_cons_self i s')]
493	    rw [ih (fun j hj => hF j (Finset.mem_cons.mpr (Or.inr hj))) r y]
494	    rw [smul_add]

496	/-- Iter-098 refactor: sum-through-composition extension of
497	`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`. The existing lemma's single morphism
498	family `F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂))` cannot absorb an intermediate
499	`eqToHom` because instantiating `F := fun i ↦ G i ≫ eqToHom`
500	re-introduces a `Fin (...) → ↑s₀` vs `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀` unification
501	problem outside tactic state (the iter-097 Attempt 5 dead-end). This
502	parallel lemma splits the morphism into a family `G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶
503	(∏ᶜ Z_int))` plus a single `E : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂)`, so the
504	`eqToHom` slot at the call site lands in `E` (an independent
505	elaboration slot) rather than inside the family.
506	
507	Body left as `sorry` for the iter-099 prover. Proof sketch:
508	`rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]` to convert
509	`(∑ G i) ≫ E` to `∑ (G i ≫ E)`, then `exact
510	alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y`.
511	Both rewrites are HOU-free at the signature level because `G` is a
512	binder, not a literal with nested-`i` Pi.lift body. -/
513	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp
514	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
515	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
516	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι_int : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u}
517	    (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
518	    (Z_int : ι_int → ModuleCat.{u} k)
519	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
520	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
521	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
522	    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
523	    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
524	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
525	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
526	    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
527	      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
528	        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
529	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
530	      e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
531	        r • e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y)) := by
532	  -- iter-099 Step 1: distribute `Preadditive.sum_comp` (HOU-free since `G`
533	  -- and `E` are lemma binders, not literals), then apply
534	  -- `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on the family `fun i ↦ G i ≫ E`.
535	  intro r y
536	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]
537	  exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y

778	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
779	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
780	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
781	schemes in our setting.
782	
783	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
784	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
785	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
786	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
787	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
788	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
789	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
790	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
791	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
792	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
793	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.

797	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
798	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## cechCofaceMap_pi_smul `?hG` discharge sorry at L768 (was L728/L820/...)

### Attempt 1: S1 — `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`
- **Approach:** Push the outer RHS `r' • _ j'` to `r' • (_ j')` via Pi.smul_apply on the post-funext goal.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. Goal shape changes from `... = (r' • _) j'` to `... = r' • _ j'`.

### Attempt 2: S2 — `show ... (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom _`
- **Approach:** Rewrite `e₂ _ j'` on both sides via `show` to `(ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j'))`.
  Direct `rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j']` did NOT fire (the lemma's discrimination pattern `(ConcreteCategory.hom (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).hom) ?x j'` doesn't match `e₂ x j'` syntactically even though they're def-equal).
- **Result:** RESOLVED via `show` (def-eq pivot).

### Attempt 3: S3 — `← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply` x4
- **Approach:** Normalize `ModuleCat.Hom.hom = ConcreteCategory.hom` then absorb 4 nested applications `(Pi.π Z₂ j') (eqToHom_hom (smul_thing.hom (e₁.symm _)))` into single categorical morphism `((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j'`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. Both LHS and RHS now have shape `(ConcreteCategory.hom (((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom ⋯ ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j')) (e₁.symm _)`. S1-S3 chain COMMITTED to file at L795-L807.

### Attempt 4: S4 — extract `(-1)^↑i •` from composition via `Preadditive.zsmul_comp`
- **Approach:** `rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` (which states `(?n • ?f) ≫ ?g = ?n • ?f ≫ ?g` for `?n : ℤ`).
- **Result:** FAILED. Same discrimination-tree class issue persists POST-S3 just as pre-funext.
  Error: "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `(?n • ?f) ≫ ?g`". The smul's underlying `f` is `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` with anonymous-closure codomain `∏ᶜ (fun i_1 ↦ ModuleCat.of k Γ(...))`, blocking unification.
- **Verification:** `((-1 : ℤ) ^ (↑i : ℕ)) • Pi.lift_thing = same from rfl` succeeds (smul IS ℤ-zsmul). `set h_sgn : ℤ := (-1)^(↑i : ℕ)` substitutes (confirms ℤ). `simp_rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` also fails. The lemma's RHS is in scope, the LHS scalar IS ℤ-typed, but the discrimination tree can't reach into the Pi.lift codomain.

### Attempt 5: body-local `have h_smul_hom (n : ℤ) (f : M ⟶ N) (x) : (n • f).hom x = n • f.hom x := by intros; rfl`
- **Approach:** Per iter-101 plan ESCALATION (E1) option — body-local helper bypassing discrimination tree.
- **Result:** FAILED. The helper TYPECHECKS (rfl proof closes it) but `simp only [h_smul_hom]` and `rw [h_smul_hom]` report "no progress". Same discrimination-tree class — the Pi.lift's anonymous-closure codomain blocks the pattern-match even for the body-local rfl-lemma.

### Attempt 6: `set Pi_lift_thing := Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...`
- **Approach:** Per iter-101 plan ESCALATION (E2) option — name the closure so the smul's RHS has a binder name rather than literal anonymous closure.
- **Result:** PARTIAL FAILURE. `set` syntactically accepts the binding but does NOT substitute the pattern in the goal (`set` substitutes by syntactic match; `(∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ _` type ascription doesn't match the actual `∏ᶜ (fun i_1 ↦ ...)` codomain in the goal). Subsequent `rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` still fails for the same reason.

## Iter-101 outcome summary

**Sorries: 6 → 6** (no net reduction). **STRUCTURAL ADVANCE**: S1-S3 of the 6-step recipe LANDED and committed. The post-S3 goal at L811 is now in the clean form `(ConcreteCategory.hom (((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom ⋯ ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j')) (e₁.symm _) = r' • (same) (e₁.symm _)` — the right shape for S4-S6 closure, but blocked by the same discrimination-tree class that defeated iter-099 + iter-100 pre-funext routes.

**Root cause confirmed**: The `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` with anonymous-closure codomain `∏ᶜ (fun i_1 ↦ ModuleCat.of k Γ(C.left, op (∏ᶜ basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1)))` blocks Lean's discrimination tree at the `(?n • ?f) ≫ ?g` / `(n • f).hom` / `f ≫ Pi.π _` / `set f := Pi.lift _` levels — regardless of whether the lemma is a Mathlib `@[simp]` rfl (`ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`, `Preadditive.zsmul_comp`), a body-local rfl helper, or a manual `set` binding. The post-funext frame DOES NOT dissolve this class as iter-100 hypothesized.

**File compiles** (verified `lean_diagnostic_messages` severity=error returns `[]`). No new axioms. S1-S3 chain at L795-L807 is durably committed and serves as the foundation for iter-102's escalation.

## STREAK ESCALATION CRITERION TRIGGERED — iter-102 MANDATE

Per iter-101 plan: "If iter-101 also stalls after 3–4 sub-attempts at LSP, the prover MUST abort and write a SHORT report. The iter-102 plan agent will then MANDATE escalation."

Six sub-attempts at LSP exhausted (S4 raw `rw`, `simp_rw`, body-local `have` rfl, body-local `set`, `Preadditive.zsmul_comp` direct rewrite, `ModuleCat.hom_zsmul` direct simp). The discrimination-tree class is structural, not tactical.

### Recommended iter-102 escalation: option E2 (refactor lane)

Both E1 (body-local helper) and the in-place `set` approach FAIL because the discrimination tree cannot pattern-match through the anonymous-closure Pi.lift codomain — this is **not** a "body-local vs top-level" issue. The class fails at ANY level when the closure has nested `i`-references.

**Concrete iter-102 refactor directive**: introduce a top-level helper that takes the Čech summand morphism family as a **single named binder** of an explicit (named-codomain) Pi-product type. Specifically:

```lean
-- Top-level helper for iter-102
theorem cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand_via_named
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {R : Type*} [Ring R]
    {ι₁ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    [Module R (∀ i, Z₁ i)] [Module R (∀ j, Z₂ j)]
    (h : ι₂ → ι₁)  -- index re-mapping per coordinate
    (T : ∀ j, Z₁ (h j) ⟶ Z₂ j)  -- per-coordinate morphism
    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
    (hT : ∀ j (r : R) (y_j : Z₁ (h j)),
      e₂ ((T j).hom (r • y_j)) = r • e₂ ((T j).hom y_j))  -- placeholder: needs proper per-coord shape
    (n : ℤ) (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i) :
    -- Per-coord R-linearity statement of `n • (Pi.lift via T) ≫ Pi.π _ j` ...
    sorry
```

The point: the family `T : ∀ j, Z₁ (h j) ⟶ Z₂ j` has a NAMED Pi-codomain at each coordinate, eliminating the anonymous-closure issue. The iter-102 prover applies it at the `?hG` discharge site via `congr` / `refine` against `T := fun j ↦ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift _).op` with `h j := j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom` peeled into a binder.

The iter-101 plan's E1 option (body-local helper) is now CONFIRMED INSUFFICIENT — six attempts at body-local routes all fail at the same discrimination-tree class. The refactor lane is the durable path.

### Alternative iter-102 escalation: option E3 (direct LinearMap.ext + funext-style)

Lean's discrimination tree fails on `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ <anonymous-i-body>`, but `LinearMap.ext`-based applications IGNORE the discrimination tree by lifting the equality to `∀ z, ... z = ... z`. Specifically:
```lean
-- inside the ?hG discharge at L811
apply LinearMap.ext_iff.mpr
intro z
-- goal: LHS z = RHS z, where both sides have explicit `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...` applied to z
-- now `simp only [Pi.lift]` or `Pi.lift_apply` can unfold the Pi.lift at the value level
```

This requires an additional `LinearMap` re-framing of the goal (since the current shape is at function-application level via `ConcreteCategory.hom`, not LinearMap level), but may be lighter than E2 if it works.

## Diff to file

S1-S3 chain (L795-L807) committed; smaller iter-101 diagnostic comments at L808-L810. Original sorry preserved at L811 with iter-102 escalation options documented inline.

## iter-099 · parallel prover

The 3rd consecutive prover lane on `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` confirms a structural Lean discrimination-tree issue, not a tactic-difficulty issue: whenever the smul operand is `Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ <body with outer-i refs>` with anonymous-closure codomain `∏ᶜ (fun i_1 ↦ ModuleCat.of k Γ(...))`, NO tactic-level rewrite of `(n • f) ≫ g`, `(n • f).hom`, or `f ≫ Pi.π _ _` can find the pattern — even when the lemma is a `@[simp]` rfl (`ModuleCat.hom_zsmul`, `Preadditive.zsmul_comp`), a body-local rfl-helper, a literal `show ... from rfl`, OR a `set f := ...` binding. This is the SAME class that iter-100 documented pre-funext; iter-101 confirms it persists post-funext at the per-coordinate `Z₂ j'` level.

A standardized Archon protocol for detecting this class early would be valuable: if the plan agent could test the lemma in vacuum (rfl works) vs in-context (rfl fails) BEFORE issuing a multi-step tactic recipe, multi-iter stalls like iter-099/100/101 on this slot could be avoided. The fast-path detection: `lean_run_code` standalone test passes + `lean_multi_attempt` in-context fails ⇒ flag as anonymous-closure discrimination-tree class ⇒ go straight to refactor-lane escalation rather than burning a prover iteration on tactic chains.
